The classification of Bredemeyera nudiuscula places it in the kingdom Plantae, with its scientific name indicating its unique characteristics. This plant is often classified using the APG III system, which groups it within the clade of dicotyledons. Bredemeyera nudiuscula is also known by its synonym, Polygala nudiuscula.

intro: Bredemeyera nudiuscula, also known as the 'nudie bush', is a plant species in the family Polygalaceae, native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. Bioactive constituents of Bredemeyera nudiuscula include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
